Looking at the image, the white figure has moved up and is now overlapping with the green frame again. The green frame pieces are embedded in the figure's body. The red feet are visible at the bottom. The figure seems to be in a more compact state now.
Let me check the segmentation to understand the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'R', 'G'):
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}, children={n['children']}")
print("\nTotal nodes:", len(seg['nodes']))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
